behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (request builder -> API client payload), not only a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network is required; a fake in-process client can capture payload. MOCK-HIDE: mocking transport does not inherently hide this failure because missing labels are visible in captured request JSON. LLM-DEP: does not depend on model judgment. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified by behavior text focused on request content inclusion, which is verifiable before real network dispatch.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outgoing requests; call classify() and assert the payload contains the full configured taxonomy list (no omissions) every time.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is direct normalization/validation logic on one response value. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: no, because the failure is in local mapping logic and is exposed by direct function input. LLM-DEP: no model-quality dependency; test injects explicit invalid label. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected higher level: Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified because behavior text is purely about mapping unexpected label to "unknown".
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/response-handling function with an out-of-taxonomy label and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: deterministic transformation property of one local normalization step. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: no; deterministic idempotence is fully testable in-process. LLM-DEP: none because raw response is fixed input fixture. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected higher level: Integration+ are disqualified by behavior text explicitly constraining to identical raw responses and normalization output determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants if applicable) and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior explicitly concerns repeated live provider behavior over real calls. REAL-INFRA: real HTTP is involved, but LLM/API decision variability is the dominant risk boundary. MOCK-HIDE: a stub would always be stable and hide real drift/variance. LLM-DEP: yes; correctness depends on what the real AI API returns across runs. MULTI-STEP: single agentic step repeated, not multi-stage pipeline. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified by behavior text "repeated live API calls" and dependence on provider-returned labels.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real classification calls with fixed document+taxonomy under controlled request params; record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label stability when raw provider response content is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at handoff between token-budget logic and dispatch decision (truncate/reject/send). REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS required; in-process wiring is enough. MOCK-HIDE: fully mocked module internals could hide it, but a fake client at boundary will expose whether over-budget requests are blocked/rewritten. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected higher level: System/Agentic are disqualified because behavior is pre-dispatch enforcement logic.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low token budget, run classifier with oversized input using real module wiring and a fake API client, and assert policy outcome (truncate payload or reject) happens before any outbound dispatch.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can arise from real provider-side token accounting differences vs local estimates. REAL-INFRA: real external call is needed. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can report in-budget usage and miss true provider usage. LLM-DEP: execution path depends on real AI API call; per rule, default Agentic unless exclusively local wiring. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified by behavior text "Provider-reported token usage" which requires real provider response metadata.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API calls on representative documents with configured budget, capture provider-reported usage fields, and assert observed usage does not exceed budget for the defined typical-input set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System is plausible, but Agentic is retained because the behavior requires a real AI API call and provider-side token behavior.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is orchestration across retry/backoff + client + error translation. REAL-INFRA: real network is not required; deterministic fake client can emit 429 then success. MOCK-HIDE: mocking only success would hide retry bug, but boundary fake that returns rate-limit exposes it. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output content. MULTI-STEP: retry loop is control-flow, not multi-agentic pipeline. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text is about handling a specific API signal, reproducible with in-process simulated responses.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client scripted to return rate-limit response then success; assert module waits according to retry policy, retries, and ultimately returns label instead of bubbling error.
